description: Semantic similarity configuration for SQL databases - Detects differences in CREATE INDEX, PROCEDURE, FUNCTION, etc. to properly classify them as separate operations when object names differ

identifierPatterns.name: SQL CREATE INDEX
identifierPatterns.description: Matches CREATE INDEX statements and captures the index name in brackets. Example: CREATE INDEX [IX_TableName_ColumnName]

identifierPatterns.name: SQL CREATE PROCEDURE/FUNCTION/VIEW/TRIGGER
identifierPatterns.description: Matches CREATE statements for stored procedures, functions, views, and triggers. Captures the object name.

identifierPatterns.name: SQL ALTER PROCEDURE/FUNCTION/VIEW/TRIGGER
identifierPatterns.description: Matches ALTER statements for database objects

identifierPatterns.name: SQL CREATE TABLE
identifierPatterns.description: Matches CREATE TABLE statements and captures table name

identifierPatterns.name: SQL CONSTRAINT Names
identifierPatterns.description: Matches CONSTRAINT definitions and captures constraint names
